shots.caption: One codebase. Offline-first. Zero backend.
shots.narration: This is Mileway — an offline-first mileage and expense tracker, one Kotlin codebase, forty-five modules.

shots.caption: Sign in, or skip it entirely
shots.narration: It starts like any app — sign in, or continue as a guest, no account required to explore.

shots.caption: Everything at a glance
shots.narration: The home screen surfaces mileage, cards and quick actions, reading straight from Room on-device — no network required.

shots.caption: Tap start to begin tracking
shots.narration: Tap start, and the location engine takes over.

shots.caption: GPS fusion, live and accurate
shots.narration: GPS fixes and accelerometer data fuse in real time, with spike detection filtering jitter so the route stays accurate even when the signal doesn't.

shots.caption: Distance to money, instantly
shots.narration: When the trip ends, the policy engine turns distance into money — reimbursement computed from per-vehicle rate rules, instantly and offline.

shots.caption: Expenses, receipts, vouchers
shots.narration: Expenses, receipts and vouchers live in the same offline data model.

shots.caption: Approvals with policy checks
shots.narration: Approvals flag policy violations automatically, before a manager ever taps approve.

shots.caption: Payables, journaled durably
shots.narration: Every submission is journaled to a durable outbox first — kill the app mid-submit, and nothing is lost or double-counted.

shots.caption: An AI assistant, fully offline
shots.narration: An on-device AI assistant answers questions over your own trips and expenses — no remote model, no server, still offline.

shots.caption: A plugin platform, per persona
shots.narration: Under it all sits a plugin-composition platform — four personas reshape the entire app from one account.

shots.caption: Phone, watch, desktop — one core
shots.narration: And the same shared Kotlin core drives the phone, the watch, and this desktop window — five platforms, one codebase, zero backend.
